note: Light-doc submodule of calendar_view. Recipe-only eval suite (no medium/hard): the submodule is zero-configuration -- it only attaches an asset library and adds CSS classes/data attributes via preprocess hooks, so there is no live config to introspect or build. All cases are response-graded facts grounded in the submodule source.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the Calendar View - Multiday (calendar_view_multiday) submodule do, and does it require any configuration to work?
evals.expected_output: It improves how events that span multiple days are rendered on a Calendar View, drawing them as one continuous bar across day cells. It is zero-configuration: enabling the module (it depends on calendar_view) is the entire setup -- there is no settings form or config entity.

evals.prompt: Which CSS classes and/or data attributes does the calendar_view_multiday submodule add to rendered results in a calendar day cell so multi-day events can be styled?
evals.expected_output: Via preprocess hooks it adds is-multi, is-multi--first, is-multi--middle, and is-multi--last classes plus data-calendar-view-instance and data-calendar-view-instances attributes to each result in a day cell, and attaches the calendar_view_multiday/multiday library.

evals.prompt: What module does calendar_view_multiday depend on, and does it define any config, permissions, or Drush commands of its own?
evals.expected_output: It depends on calendar_view (it is a submodule of that project). It defines no config schema/entity, no permissions, and no Drush commands -- it only attaches an asset library and adds markup via preprocess hooks.
